- There's a new white cross at (12, 24) with 10 pixels (id=9) - this is where the orange cross was before
So clicking the magenta center moves the orange cross along the path. The orange cross moved from (12, 24) to (14, 27), which is further along the path towards the gray cross at (9, 49).

- Gray crosses at (21, 8), (35, 45), (48, 54)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Orange cross moved to (14, 27). Continue moving it along the path.
# Click magenta center at (14, 27) to move it further

# Let me first check the current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current crosses:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['O', 'p', 'G', 'W'] and 10 <= n['pixels'] <= 25:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']))
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} center~{center} children={n['children']}")

# Click magenta center of orange cross at (14, 27)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 14, 'col': 27}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter click:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['O', 'p', 'G', 'W'] and 10 <= n['pixels'] <= 25:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']))
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} center~{center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
